model.label: Claude Opus 4.6
model.description: Anthropic • Hybrid reasoning model that pushes the frontier for coding and AI agents, featuring a 1M context window.

pick.rationale: Duke and Michigan are the two best teams in the country with nearly identical statistical profiles, but Duke holds a narrow edge as the #1 overall team with a 0.79-point higher adjusted efficiency margin (+39.17 vs +38.38), a marginally superior offense (AdjO 127.9 vs 126.7), and the critical head-to-head advantage—having already beaten Michigan 68-63 on a neutral court in February behind Cameron Boozer's 18 points while Michigan shot poorly from three. While Duke is missing starting point guard Caleb Foster and Isaiah Evans, they have adapted throughout the tournament with Cayden Boozer stepping up and Cooper Flagg performing at a generational level, and Michigan's Yaxel Lendeborg is playing through a low ankle sprain that could limit his effectiveness in a game this tight. In what projects as one of the closest national championship games in recent memory between the #1 and #2 teams in the country, Duke's fractional statistical superiority, proven head-to-head result, and Cooper Flagg's transcendent talent tip the balance narrowly in the Blue Devils' favor.

reasoningStep.title: National Championship: Duke vs Michigan
reasoningStep.summary: Duke edges Michigan in a razor-thin matchup between the nation's #1 and #2 teams, supported by a 0.79-point adjusted efficiency margin advantage, a February head-to-head neutral-court victory, and Cooper Flagg's generational talent, despite missing Caleb Foster and Isaiah Evans.
evidence: Duke KenPom #1 (AdjEM +39.17, AdjO 127.9 4th, AdjD 88.7 1st) vs Michigan KenPom #2 (AdjEM +38.38, AdjO 126.7 8th, AdjD 88.4 1st)
Duke Torvik #1 (AdjOE 128.0, AdjDE 90.4) vs Michigan Torvik #2 (AdjOE 127.7, AdjDE 90.5)
Both teams 31-2 records; Duke beat Michigan 68-63 on neutral court Feb 21, 2026
Duke missing Caleb Foster (foot surgery) and Isaiah Evans (ACC Tournament injury); Michigan's Yaxel Lendeborg playing through low ankle sprain from Big Ten Championship
Cooper Flagg is projected #1 NBA pick performing at elite level; Cameron Boozer scored 18 in head-to-head win over Michigan
Michigan built via high-impact transfers (Cadeau, Mara, Lendeborg, Johnson Jr.) under Dusty May; Duke's depth has adapted well without Foster throughout tournament run

result.text: Duke 68-63 Michigan (Feb 21, 2026) Game Recap - ESPN Skip to main content Skip to navigation ESPN NFL NFL NBA NBA NCAAM NCAAM NCAAW NCAAW MLB MLB NHL NHL Soccer Soccer More Sports More Sports Watch Watch Fantasy Fantasy Where to Watch Search Duke Blue Devils vs Michigan Wolverines Edward Jones Capital Showcase 1 Michigan Wolverines Michigan MICH 25-2 63 25-2 Final 1 2 T Michigan MICH 33 30 63 Duke DUKE 35 33 68 3 Duke Blue Devils Duke DUKE 25-2 68 25-2 Gamecast Recap Box Score Play-by-Play Team Stats Videos Cameron Boozer and No. 3 Duke knock off No. 1 Michigan 68-63 as top 2 teams lose on same day By AP Updated: Feb 21, 2026, 09:05 pm Facebook Facebook Messenger Twitter Email WASHINGTON -- — Cameron Boozer scored 18 points and No. 3 Duke handed another neutral-site loss to fellow blueblood Michigan , knocking off the top-ranked Wolverines 68-63 on Saturday night in a raucous possible Final Four preview in the nation's capital. The Wolverines had won 11 straight and replaced Arizona atop the AP Top 25 this week. The now-fourth-ranked Wildcats won at No. 2 Houston earlier Saturday, making it the first time since Feb. 8, 2025, that the teams ranked Nos. 1 and 2 in the poll lost on the same day. With his dad, former Duke and NBA star Carlos Boozer, in attendance, Cameron Boozer hit a 3-pointer with 1:55 left to give the Blue Devils a 64-58 lead. Isaiah Evans added 14 points for the Blue Devils (25-2), who could return to Capital One Arena in just over a month for the East Regional of the NCAA Tournament. In the programs' first meeting in 12 years, Duke improved to 23-8 against Michigan and 7-0 on neutral courts, a series that includes the 1992 national title game. Michigan hasn't beaten Duke since Dec. 6, 2009 in Ann Arbor. Yaxel Lendeborg scored 21 points for the Wolverines (25-2), who had not lost since a home defeat by three points to Wisconsin on Jan. 10. While second-year coach Dusty May’s Michigan squad has been hailed for unselfishness, Jon Scheyer’s Blue Devils showed more versatility on the offensive end. Caleb Foster scored 12 points and Patrick Ngongba II added 11 as Duke found soft spots in the Wolverines’ top-rated defense. Meanwhile, Duke won the rebounding battle 41-28 as Michigan settled for too many one-and-done perimeter shots, going 6 of 25 from 3-point range. Fans traded chants of “Let’s go Duke!” and “Let’s go Blue!” for the rare must-see matchup in a city starved for meaningful basketball. ESPN’s “College GameDay” was broadcast from inside the arena during the teams’ morning shootaround, and tickets for upper-level seats were selling in the $600 range in the hours before the game, with courtside seats upwards of $6,000. Defense had the upper hand in a spirited, physical first half in which neither team led by more than five points. Ngongba was fouled while fighting for a rebound with 0.8 seconds left and made both free throws to give the Blue Devils a 35-33 lead at the break. Surprisingly, Duke never trailed again. Up next Mich

arguments.query: Duke national championship 2026 Cooper Flagg Cayden Boozer

result.text: Duke 68-63 Michigan (Feb 21, 2026) Game Recap - ESPN Skip to main content Skip to navigation ESPN NFL NFL NBA NBA NCAAM NCAAM NCAAW NCAAW MLB MLB NHL NHL Soccer Soccer More Sports More Sports Watch Watch Fantasy Fantasy Where to Watch Search Duke Blue Devils vs Michigan Wolverines Edward Jones Capital Showcase 1 Michigan Wolverines Michigan MICH 25-2 63 25-2 Final 1 2 T Michigan MICH 33 30 63 Duke DUKE 35 33 68 3 Duke Blue Devils Duke DUKE 25-2 68 25-2 Gamecast Recap Box Score Play-by-Play Team Stats Videos Cameron Boozer and No. 3 Duke knock off No. 1 Michigan 68-63 as top 2 teams lose on same day By AP Updated: Feb 21, 2026, 09:05 pm Facebook Facebook Messenger Twitter Email WASHINGTON -- — Cameron Boozer scored 18 points and No. 3 Duke handed another neutral-site loss to fellow blueblood Michigan , knocking off the top-ranked Wolverines 68-63 on Saturday night in a raucous possible Final Four preview in the nation's capital. The Wolverines had won 11 straight and replaced Arizona atop the AP Top 25 this week. The now-fourth-ranked Wildcats won at No. 2 Houston earlier Saturday, making it the first time since Feb. 8, 2025, that the teams ranked Nos. 1 and 2 in the poll lost on the same day. With his dad, former Duke and NBA star Carlos Boozer, in attendance, Cameron Boozer hit a 3-pointer with 1:55 left to give the Blue Devils a 64-58 lead. Isaiah Evans added 14 points for the Blue Devils (25-2), who could return to Capital One Arena in just over a month for the East Regional of the NCAA Tournament. In the programs' first meeting in 12 years, Duke improved to 23-8 against Michigan and 7-0 on neutral courts, a series that includes the 1992 national title game. Michigan hasn't beaten Duke since Dec. 6, 2009 in Ann Arbor. Yaxel Lendeborg scored 21 points for the Wolverines (25-2), who had not lost since a home defeat by three points to Wisconsin on Jan. 10. While second-year coach Dusty May’s Michigan squad has been hailed for unselfishness, Jon Scheyer’s Blue Devils showed more versatility on the offensive end. Caleb Foster scored 12 points and Patrick Ngongba II added 11 as Duke found soft spots in the Wolverines’ top-rated defense. Meanwhile, Duke won the rebounding battle 41-28 as Michigan settled for too many one-and-done perimeter shots, going 6 of 25 from 3-point range. Fans traded chants of “Let’s go Duke!” and “Let’s go Blue!” for the rare must-see matchup in a city starved for meaningful basketball. ESPN’s “College GameDay” was broadcast from inside the arena during the teams’ morning shootaround, and tickets for upper-level seats were selling in the $600 range in the hours before the game, with courtside seats upwards of $6,000. Defense had the upper hand in a spirited, physical first half in which neither team led by more than five points. Ngongba was fouled while fighting for a rebound with 0.8 seconds left and made both free throws to give the Blue Devils a 35-33 lead at the break. Surprisingly, Duke never trailed again. Up next Mich
